creator: jbeich@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Henryk Paluch from comment #2)
> I suggest to ensure that make.conf on package build server

See ---Begin make.conf--- in https://pkg-status.freebsd.org/beefy22/data/latest-per-pkg/ncdu2/2.4_1/140amd64-default.log

Zig compiler itself targets native CPU by default. Contrary to compilers for every other programming language. There's not much to do other than explicitly pass -Dcpu= in every Zig-based port.

> generic x86_64 CPUs.

FreeBSD 13.0 promoted aarch64 to Tier1 and demoted i386 to Tier2. lang/zig is currently supported on both amd64 and aarch64, so maintainers cannot disregard other architectures so quikcly.

See also
https://www.freebsd.org/platforms/
https://www.freebsd.org/internal/machines/
